description: Event name. Always "relink_guess_submitted" for this event.
The game emitting the event. Always "relink".

Which phase of the game the guess belongs to. Relink has two phases per puzzle: (1) "imposters" — for each of the 4 themed rows, the player picks the single word that does NOT belong to that row's connection (the "imposter"). (2) "relink" — after all imposters are correctly identified, the imposters together form a new mini-connection; the player picks 1–4 tiles that spell out that final link. Use this field to split the funnel.
Whether THIS guess was correct. For "imposters" that means the picked word matches the row's imposter; for "relink" that means the selected tiles match the final answer set (order-independent).

Lives left AFTER this guess is resolved. Players start the puzzle with 4 attempts (TOTAL_ATTEMPTS=4). A correct guess does NOT consume an attempt, so attempts_remaining is unchanged. A wrong guess costs 1 attempt. Range: 0–4. When this reaches 0 on a wrong guess, the game is lost. The counter is shared across both phases (a wrong imposter pick and a wrong relink pick both decrement the same pool).
Which of the 4 themed rows the guess was made on. 0-indexed, top-to-bottom as shown on the board. Rows are presented in a fixed order per puzzle. Absent for phase="relink" guesses.
The exact word text the player tapped as the suspected imposter. Uppercased as displayed on the tile. Absent for phase="relink" guesses.

Comma-separated list of tile IDs the player submitted as their final link guess, in the order the player placed them into the answer slots. Tile ID format is "r{rowIndex}w{wordIndex}": "r" + the 0-indexed row (0–3) + "w" + the 0-indexed word position within that row (0–3). The same tile ID always refers to the same board position within a given puzzle, so you can join guesses against the puzzle's word layout to recover which words were picked. Length is always = number of answer slots (1–4, set per puzzle). Absent for phase="imposters" guesses.
